name: transportation-assistance
description: Subsidized transit, vehicle repair assistance, ride programs, and transportation barriers to employment and education. The invisible wall.
source: Liberation Labs mutual aid knowledge base

triples.subject: Medicaid NEMT
triples.object: free rides to medical appointments for Medicaid recipients — Non-Emergency Medical Transportation, arranged through Medicaid managed care plan

triples.subject: workforce development board
triples.object: transit passes for participants in approved training programs — ask when enrolling in workforce services

triples.subject: vehicle repair charity
triples.object: free or low-cost repairs for low-income car owners — programs like Working Cars for Working People exist in many areas

triples.subject: ADA paratransit
triples.object: any area with fixed-route public transit — door-to-door service for people unable to use regular buses/trains

triples.subject: 211 transportation
triples.object: local volunteer driver programs, subsidized transit, rideshare vouchers, and transportation assistance funds

triples.subject: car donation program
triples.object: donated vehicles to qualifying low-income individuals — programs like 1-800-Charity-Cars, Vehicles for Change, and local programs

triples.subject: reduced-fare transit pass
triples.object: seniors, people with disabilities, Medicare cardholders, and sometimes students and low-income riders

triples.subject: employer transportation benefit
triples.object: pre-tax transit passes ($315/month max), vanpool subsidies, or bicycle commuter benefits

triples.subject: JARC (Job Access and Reverse Commute)
triples.object: transit services connecting low-income workers to suburban job sites — operated by local transit agencies

triples.subject: bike share/lending library
triples.object: free or low-cost bicycle access — many cities have income-qualified free memberships for bike share systems

triples.subject: gas assistance
triples.object: Salvation Army, St. Vincent de Paul, community action agencies, and some TANF emergency assistance programs

triples.subject: driver's license recovery
triples.object: programs to clear license suspensions for unpaid fines/fees — many states now limit license suspension for non-driving debts

triples.subject: insurance assistance
triples.object: state minimum liability only, pay-per-mile programs (Metromile), and low-cost auto insurance programs in some states

triples.subject: carpooling
triples.object: employer-sponsored vanpools, community ride boards, and apps like Waze Carpool — reduces cost and provides backup transportation

triples.subject: rural transportation
triples.object: rural transit systems (Section 5311 funded), volunteer driver programs, faith-based transportation ministries, and telehealth as transportation substitute

triples.subject: school transportation
triples.object: free bus service within district boundaries, McKinney-Vento transportation for homeless students regardless of district, and IEP transportation for eligible students

triples.subject: transportation to court
triples.object: legal aid organizations, court social workers, or community organizations — missing court for transportation reasons can result in warrants

triples.subject: transportation barrier
triples.object: benefits applications, reasonable accommodation requests, and job interview scheduling — it's a recognized barrier that programs are designed to address

triples.subject: electric vehicle assistance
triples.object: federal tax credit up to $7,500, state rebates, and used EV tax credit up to $4,000 — some programs target low-income buyers

triples.subject: transportation planning
triples.object: map all options (transit, bike, carpool, walk, telecommute) before assuming a car is necessary — total car ownership cost often exceeds $500/month
